name: CurveBurner
report: The attack exploited a compiler-level vulnerability in Vyper (versions 0.2.15–0.3.0) which caused the `@nonreentrant('lock')` decorator to malfunction. This flaw assigned different storage slots for the reentrancy lock in different functions (e.g., `add_liquidity` and `remove_liquidity`), rendering the lock ineffective. Attackers targeted Curve pools containing ETH, as the `remove_liquidity` function uses a low-level `raw_call` to send ETH before updating the total supply. This allowed reentrant calls to `add_liquidity` within the same transaction, minting excessive LP tokens at an artificially low price due to the inconsistent state, ultimately draining multiple pools of their funds.
